topic.what: Dictum 'No acid, no ulcer' that defined 20th-century gastroenterology and directed all treatment toward acid suppression.
topic.how: Clinical observation that acid is necessary for peptic ulceration, generalized into an exclusive etiological framework.
topic.why_matters: One of medicine's most consequential partially-correct insights — directed research productively toward PPIs but also blocked bacterial gastritis investigation for 70+ years.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Years as dominant gastric disease paradigm
characteristics.vs_baseline: Replaced humoral theory, itself replaced by H. pylori

good_for: Understanding how partial truths become paradigm barriers
Historical context for PPI development and H. pylori resistance

novelty: Correctly identified acid as necessary condition for ulceration
Directed development of antacids, H2 blockers, and PPIs
open_problems: Mistook necessary condition for sufficient condition
Delayed H. pylori acceptance by at least a decade after Marshall-Warren
